description_long: This object is a compact, pump-action shotgun with a short barrel and a wooden or polymer stock that curves downward, giving it a distinctive, sawed-off appearance. The receiver and barrel are constructed from dark, matte-finished metal, showing signs of wear and use, while the fore-end features a ribbed grip for manual cycling. The stock is a faded brownish-green, likely made from wood or a synthetic material, and the trigger guard is integrated into the main body. A detachable box magazine is visible beneath the receiver, indicating a modernized design. The muzzle is plain, with no visible attachments, and the overall form is utilitarian, suggesting use in close-quarters urban environments. There are no advanced optics or rail systems, only basic iron sights for aiming.
description: A compact, pump-action shotgun with a short barrel, ribbed fore-end, and a curved, worn stock. Features a detachable box magazine and basic iron sights.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, curved stock, ribbed fore-end, and detachable box magazine.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the barrel opening and the ribbed fore-end grip.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the stock curve, magazine, and pump-action fore-end.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the width of the stock and the alignment of the barrel and receiver.
